The Vault v0.6.0 introduces the Open-Loops v2 foundation — typed Work and Brain projects with a dedicated, evidence-driven loop lifecycle — and makes the AI runtime cockpit truthful about providers.

Highlights:
- Work and Brain project types: every new project is created explicitly as a Work Project (executable commitments) or a Brain / Memory-Context project (durable knowledge). Brain projects can never own open loops — enforced in core services and by SQLite triggers — and legacy projects stay `unclassified` until governed classification; names are never used to infer a type.
- Dedicated open-loop lifecycle (shadow mode): strictly admitted loops with mandatory admission fields and dedupe, an explicit state machine, evidence-backed outcome-specific resolution, governed owner/role/quorum/external snooze approvals with exact-state resume on expiry, governed recovery, and append-only event/approval audit trails — every mutation idempotent, versioned, and transactional.
- Deterministic per-project gate: gate evaluation reports blockers, reason codes, and allowed work intents with a full audit trail. Shadow mode only — nothing blocks real work yet; enforcement remains a later per-project governed cutover.
- Additive migration with safeguards: pre-migration SQLite backup, byte-for-byte Markdown export backup, immutable count/hash inventory, idempotent reruns, and a migration ledger. Legacy data, loops, and evidence stay untouched; shadow telemetry compares legacy-derived and dedicated counts.
- New v2 MCP tools and desktop surfaces: dedicated open-loops operations for connected agents, explicit type-specific project creation, and legacy-vs-dedicated shadow telemetry with enforcement clearly shown as off.
- Truthful AI runtime cockpit: the Agent page now shows the real primary and fallback providers and the primary provider's own enrichment model instead of a hardcoded OpenRouter label, with provider-neutral copy throughout.
- Provider failover observability: when the primary AI provider fails over, task results and executor events now record which provider failed, the models tried, the error, and the timestamp — visible in task detail and the events feed.

This build packages the desktop app and the bundled `vault-memory` MCP sidecar runtime. After installing, open The Vault and use Settings -> Client setup to connect Codex, Claude Desktop, or Claude Code.
